trivia_bullets.text: Printed in Core Set 2021 (M21), released July 3, 2020. M21 marked Prowess's return as a deciduous keyword (available to any set going forward) after its first appearance in Khans of Tarkir (2014). Red-blue spell-slinger was one of M21's ten explicit limited archetypes; Goblin Wizardry fed that lane by producing prowess-bearing tokens.
sources: Hipsters of the Coast: Core Set 2021 Limited Preview (2020)

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: '"Quiet in the library" soon gave way to larger concerns.' — unattributed [Scryfall]. Dry understated humor used as flavor; the phrasing suggests a chronicler watching events escalate beyond simple noise complaints, matching the library-destruction scene in the art.

Creates the Goblin Wizard creature type, a combination that does not otherwise appear frequently in MTG. The original Goblin Wizard creature predates this card — it first appeared in The Dark (1994). Artist credited as 'Izzy' on Scryfall — pen name of illustrator Izzy Medrano, who has extensive MTG card credits across multiple sets.
Legal in Pauper and Pauper Commander formats, where token generation paired with cheap cantrips creates consistent damage. Reprinted in The List (August 2024). EDHREC rank 13,300.

related_cards.name: Goblin Wizard
related_cards.note: The original Goblin Wizard creature; the tokens Goblin Wizardry creates carry its name
